text: 🐻 Grab a coffee or a snack and catch up on the key news of the past 24 hours! ➡️ The FSB thwarted a terrorist attack against a Kursk region official ➡️ Iranian embassies troll Trump ➡️ Israel's Ambassador to the UN claims they have the legitimacy to target civilian targets in Iran ➡️ The head of the intelligence of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps Majid Hademi was killed in US and Israeli strikes ➡️ The International Energy Agency outlines a two-track response to the Middle East oil shock (spoiler - people will have to make sacrifices) ➡️ Trump once again makes a bizarre claim that they had a "total regime change" in Iran , says the Iranian people want to hear bombs , and calls Iranians animals , while Melania says that they are bombing children "for their future" - all in the spirit of Easter, of course! ➡️ Iranian Army published satellite images of the impact of army drones on Al-Adairi base ➡️ The chair of the House Armed Services Military Personnel subcommittee, Rep. Pat Fallon, says US ground troops are coming to Iran ➡️ Trump makes a statement on the "search and rescue" operation that makes us think it definitely wasn't that , and says his team will hunt down whoever leaked claims about a second missing US F-15E pilot on Friday. Oh - and we got more pictures of the site ➡️ There was an attack on Jubail, a major industrial and port city on the eastern coast of Saudi Arabia ➡️ NASA FIRMS image suggests multiple fires at the ADNOC (Abu Dhabi National Oil Company) facility in Habshan. ➡️ TPP-4 has been completely destroyed in Kiev, says the head of the Desnyanskyi district administration Bahmatov. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

One of General Atomics’ YFQ-42A drone test aircraft crashed shortly after takeoff Monday in the California desert. The company has paused test flights for now, is inspecting the aircraft, and is investigating the cause before resuming operations. It says this is too early to speculate.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Israeli media's attempt to justify attacks on Iran's infrastructure: Iran started it first! In the Ramadan war, Israelis officially attacked Iran’s Asaluyeh oil industry facilities for the first time. Before that, attacks on civilian areas had occurred frequently, but Asaluyeh was the first case that Israel referred to as an attack on infrastructure.
A BBC report quotes an Iranian in Tehran saying he would accept even extreme outcomes of the war, including nuclear attack or total destruction: “I’m OK with all of these.”
